description: The bird was working in a patch of green algae away from the water line about 10-12 feet. It would walk and pick up bits in a typical shorebird type motion. The bird was light tannish buff overall and slightly larger than the Bairds Sandpipers that foraged right at the waters edge. The pictures showed the bird to have yellowish legs covered with the green algae and mud. The pictures also showed a shorebird with a longish neck and a somewhat small head. The shortish bill was black and quite straight. The pictures also showed a bird with a scaly pattern on the wing coverts and scapulars with dark feather centers and whitish-buff edging. The greater coverts had a buffy center to the feathers rather than the dark centers as on the other coverts. There were a series of small darker spots to the sides of the neck that came down to the upper breast and faded away at the center of the breast. The face and neck were tannish-buff, slightly darker than the underparts. The underparts were very light buff with no appreciable markings. the lower belly and vent areas were lighter than the breast area. The tertials were darker and extended past the tail tip. The detailed description of the feathers and bill and legs came from the pictures that Jack Bushong took.
